content.full_text:
Re
Fern PolrrrcAr- PnACTTcES ConrMrssroN
zl28 J Streer o Suire 620 r Sacramenro, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 12,2009
Andreas Borgeas
Councilmember for District Two
2600 Fresno Street, 2nd Floor
Fresno, California 937 2l -3600
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-09-121
Dear Councilmember Borgeas :
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
QUESTTONS
If no public moneys will be used to produce or distribute an insert in a
subscription newspaper, will the Act's mass mailing prohibition apply to the insert if it is
on official city letterhead?
CONCLUSION
If no public moneys are used to produce or distribute an insert to a subscription
newspaper, the mass mailing prohibition will not apply to the insert, even if it is on
official city letterhead.
FACTS
You are a city councilmember representing Council District Two in the City of
Fresno. You plan to start distributing newsletters by electronic mail to communicate to
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 I 000 through 9 I 0 14. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
PoliticalPractices commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-09-l2l
Page No. 2
your constituents about the activities and events pertaining to your council district. You
would like to have an insert placed in the Fresno Bee newspaper to announce that your
district newsletter will be available in electronic format. You will not be using pubti"
funds for the insert. The insert will be on official city letterhead and include your
photograph. You plan to use one piece of city letterhead to make an original insert and
then provide that to the Fresno Bee for mass distribution in the newspaper. The insert
will contain language that anyone interested in subscribing to the e-newsletters should
submit their e-mail address to the council district office. The Fresno Bee will limit the
distribution of the inserts to subscribers residing in your council district by zip code. you
will pay the newspaper for all costs of the distribution from your own funds. Based on
these facts, you would like clarification on the application of the mass mailing provisions
of the Act.
ANALYSIS
Section 89001 states that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be
sent at public expense." Regulation 18901(a) states that "except as provided in
subdivision (b), a mailing is prohibited by Section 89001 if the following criteria
are met:
"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the
recipient at his or her residence, place of employment or
business, or post office box...
(2) The item sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other
reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared or
sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert
with the elected officer.
(3)(A) Any of the cosrs of distribution is paid for with
public moneys;or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding
$50.00 are paid with public moneys, and the design,
production, or printing is done with the intent of sending
the item other than as permitted by this regulation.


File No. A-09-121
Page No. 3
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are
sent, in a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in
response to an unsolicited request and any item described
in subdivision (b)."
If public funds are not used to pay costs of design, production, printing, or
distribution, the mass mailing restrictions do not apply. Furthermore, Regulaiion 18901
provides that if the costs of distribution are not paid with public funds, and if no more
than $50 of public funds are used to pay costs of design, production or printing, the mass
mailing is not considered to have been sent at public expense. (Regulation tggOt(axf ).)
Because you indicated that public funds would not be uied to produce or distribute your
newspaper insert, with the exception of using one sheet of city letterhead,2 the mass
mailing would not be sent at public expense and the Act's mass mailing provisions do not
apply to your newspaper insert. Additionally, since the insert in the newspaper relates to
your duties of holding public office, the insert must be paid for with funds from your
campaign account. (Section 85201, Regulation 18525.)
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
NuULP- B,v-'*
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
SKBjgl
' We assume the cost of one sheet of city letterhead is under $50.

sections.question:
If no public moneys will be used to produce or distribute an insert in a
subscription newspaper, will the Act's mass mailing prohibition apply to the insert if it is
on official city letterhead?
sections.conclusion:
If no public moneys are used to produce or distribute an insert to a subscription
newspaper, the mass mailing prohibition will not apply to the insert, even if it is on
official city letterhead.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If no public moneys will be used to produce or distribute an insert in a
subscription newspaper, will the Act's mass mailing prohibition apply to the insert if it is
on official city letterhead?

CONCLUSION: If no public moneys are used to produce or distribute an insert to a subscription
newspaper, the mass mailing prohibition will not apply to the insert, even if it is on
official city letterhead.
